1.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
1.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

1.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 900 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Tri Durability — Anatomical Adaptation B (Hinge/Vertical Press/Pull)
1.description: Base Week 1 (AA phase) — Full-body tissue work. Light loads, high reps (20-25), slow tempos. Loads should feel easy; the goal is connective-tissue resilience, not muscle fatigue. RIR 3-4 throughout. Equipment — Required: Bands.

strength_exercises.name: Calf Raises (Bilateral)
strength_exercises.notes: Achilles + plantar fascia resilience for run volume

1.name: Run Intervals — 4×1000m
1.description: Warm up 10 min easy. 4×1000m at 10K / tempo pace with 90 sec jog recovery between. Cool down 10 min. Focus on consistent splits, not all-out.

1.name: Swim Threshold — 1100 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
1.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

1.name: Long Ride — 2.3 hr
1.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

1.name: Long Run — 10 mi
1.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

2.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min

9.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 1000 yd

9.name: Tri Durability — Strength Maintenance B
9.description: Build Week 9 (SM phase) — Maintain strength built in MS with 2 sets at 8-10 reps and moderate load (~70% 1RM). Movement quality, not maximal effort.  Equipment — Required: Dumbbells, Pull-up Bar, Bands.

9.name: VO2max Run — 5×3 min
9.description: Warm up 10 min easy. 5×3 min at Z5 (hard — controlled sprint, not all-out) with 90 sec float recovery. Cool down 10 min. Builds raw aerobic ceiling.

9.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 1200 yd
9.description: Warm up 300 yd. 7×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (15 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd. If you don't have a 100yd pace baseline yet, swim at an effort where you can hold a short conversation but feel like you're working. Aim for the same effort on every repeat — consistency matters more than hitting a specific number.

9.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
9.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

9.name: Brick — Bike 2.0 hr

9.name: Long Run — 10 mi
9.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

10.name: Bike Threshold — 3×20 min
10.description: Warm up 15 min with fast-pedal spins. 3×20 min at FTP (Zone 4 — hard but sustainable). 5 min easy between. Cool down 10 min.

10.name: Swim Time Trial — 1400 yd
10.description: Moderate sustained effort (comfortably hard). Warm up 500 yd with progressive build (last 100 at moderate effort). 400 yd MAX effort — sustained hard, hold form. 4 min easy recovery (back-and-forth jogs or float). 200 yd MAX effort — leave nothing. Cool down 300 yd easy. The engine will recompute your 100yd pace target from these two splits.

10.name: Tri Durability — Strength Maintenance B
10.description: Build Week 10 (SM phase) — Maintain strength built in MS with 2 sets at 8-10 reps and moderate load (~70% 1RM). Movement quality, not maximal effort.  Equipment — Required: Dumbbells, Pull-up Bar, Bands.

12.name: Easy Run — 3 mi

12.name: Easy Swim — 1050 yd
12.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Sighting Drill (head-up race-day navigation). 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

12.name: Easy Run — 2 mi
12.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

12.name: Long Ride — 1.0 hr
12.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

12.name: Long Run — 2.9 mi
12.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

13.name: Bike VO2max — 4×5 min
13.description: Warm up 15 min. 4×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

13.name: Open Water Skills — 2000 yd
13.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Open water if accessible — otherwise pool with sighting every 6 strokes throughout the main set. 4×100 yd at race-start hard effort (settle into race pace by the end of each 100), 30 sec rest. 6×200 yd at race-rhythm sustained effort with sighting every 6 strokes — bilateral breathing alternating sides. Cool down 200 yd easy. If you have access to a group, practice both lead position and drafting (feet/hip-side).

13.name: Tri Durability — Strength Maintenance B (Reduced)
13.description: Race-Specific Week 13 (SM phase) — Maintain strength built in MS with 2 sets at 8-10 reps and moderate load (~70% 1RM). Movement quality, not maximal effort. Race-specific volume reduced — minimize fatigue ahead of the race. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells, Pull-up Bar, Bands.

13.name: Half-Marathon Pace Run — 4 mi
13.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 4 mi at 70.3 / half-marathon run pace (Z3–Z4), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

13.name: Swim Threshold — 1300 yd
13.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

13.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
13.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

13.name: Brick — Bike 2.0 hr
13.description: Brick bike at Zone 2. Build leg feel for the transition. Maintain steady power throughout.

13.name: Brick — Run 2.5 mi off the bike
13.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Easy Z2 throughout.

13.name: Half-Marathon Pace Run — 4 mi
13.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 4 mi at 70.3 / half-marathon run pace (Z3–Z4), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

14.name: Bike VO2max — 5×5 min
14.description: Warm up 15 min. 5×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

14.name: Swim Time Trial — 1400 yd
14.description: Moderate sustained effort (comfortably hard). Warm up 500 yd with progressive build (last 100 at moderate effort). 400 yd MAX effort — sustained hard, hold form. 4 min easy recovery (back-and-forth jogs or float). 200 yd MAX effort — leave nothing. Cool down 300 yd easy. The engine will recompute your 100yd pace target from these two splits.

14.name: Tri Durability — Strength Maintenance B (Reduced)
14.description: Race-Specific Week 14 (SM phase) — Maintain strength built in MS with 2 sets at 8-10 reps and moderate load (~70% 1RM). Movement quality, not maximal effort. Race-specific volume reduced — minimize fatigue ahead of the race. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells, Pull-up Bar, Bands.

14.name: Tempo Run — 3 mi at threshold
14.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at lactate threshold (comfortably hard — 7–8 RPE, can say a few words). Cool down 1.5 mi easy.

14.name: Swim Threshold — 1300 yd
14.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Sighting Drill (head-up race-day navigation). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

14.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
14.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

14.name: Brick — Bike 2.3 hr
14.description: Race-simulation brick — ride 101 min at Zone 2 to build durability, then close with 34 min at expected race power (Z3, ~0.78-0.82 IF for 70.3 / 0.62-0.68 IF for full IM). Stay aero through the close. Transition quickly into the run.

14.name: Brick — Run 4.5 mi off the bike
14.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Target race pace last half.

14.name: Long Run — 10 mi
14.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

15.name: Bike VO2max — 6×5 min
15.description: Warm up 15 min. 6×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

15.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 1100 yd
15.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd. Bilateral breathing on at least half the repeats (alternate sides every 3rd or 5th stroke) so race-day chop or sun glare doesn't dictate which side you're stuck on. If you have access to swim with a group, practice both lead (no draft) and feet/hip-side draft positions — drafting can save ~10–15% of your effort on race day.

15.name: Tri Durability — Strength Maintenance B (Reduced)
15.description: Race-Specific Week 15 (SM phase) — Maintain strength built in MS with 2 sets at 8-10 reps and moderate load (~70% 1RM). Movement quality, not maximal effort. Race-specific volume reduced — minimize fatigue ahead of the race. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells, Pull-up Bar, Bands.

15.name: Tempo Run — 3 mi at threshold
15.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at lactate threshold (comfortably hard — 7–8 RPE, can say a few words). Cool down 1.5 mi easy.

15.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 1100 yd
15.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing); 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery). 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

15.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
15.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

15.name: Brick — Bike 2.5 hr
15.description: Race-simulation brick — ride 112 min at Zone 2 to build durability, then close with 38 min at expected race power (Z3, ~0.78-0.82 IF for 70.3 / 0.62-0.68 IF for full IM). Stay aero through the close. Transition quickly into the run.

15.name: Brick — Run 5 mi off the bike
15.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Target race pace last half.

15.name: Long Run — 12 mi
15.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

16.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
16.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

16.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 800 yd
16.description: Warm up 300 yd. 5×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

16.name: Tri Durability — Taper Light Session
16.description: Race week — one light session early in the week (Monday or Tuesday). 30 minutes, bodyweight or 40% 1RM, mobility focus. Skip-optional if you feel fresh and want the day for recovery. After this session, no strength until the post-race recovery week. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells, Bands.

16.name: Easy Run + Strides — 4 mi
16.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

16.name: Swim Threshold — 800 yd
16.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

16.name: Brick — Bike 0.8 hr
16.description: Brick bike at Zone 2. Build leg feel for the transition. Maintain steady power throughout.

16.name: Brick — Run 3 mi off the bike
16.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Easy Z2 throughout.

16.name: Long Run — 2.9 mi
16.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

17.name: Bike Openers — 30 min
17.description: Short pre-race sharpener. 20 min easy Z2, then 3×30-second fast-pedal bursts. Legs should feel snappy afterwards.

17.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 800 yd
17.description: Warm up 300 yd. 5×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

17.name: Tri Durability — Taper Light Session
17.description: Race week — one light session early in the week (Monday or Tuesday). 30 minutes, bodyweight or 40% 1RM, mobility focus. Skip-optional if you feel fresh and want the day for recovery. After this session, no strength until the post-race recovery week. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells, Bands.

17.name: Easy Run — 4 mi
17.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

17.name: Race-Week Activation Swim — 800 yd
17.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×50 yd build accelerations (start easy, finish strong; long rest between — pure neuromuscular sharpener, NOT a hard interval). 2×100 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

17.name: Test 70.3 race
17.description: Race day. Swim 1.2mi → Bike 56mi → Run 13.1mi. No add-on training; execute pacing and fueling.

9: Week 9 long ride is shorter than the typical build floor (2h vs 2.25h). Lengthening toward 2.25h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
13: Week 13 has no long run scheduled — race-specific plans target a 10+ mi long run for durability.
